mnemonics.title: C-P-M-C-D Sequence
mnemonics.content: Remember framework activities with: Communication → Planning → Modeling → Construction → Deployment. Think of it as: Customers talk, Project is planned, Models are built, Code is constructed, Done and deployed!

mnemonics.title: PLaMCoD for Planning Activities
mnemonics.content: Planning includes: Project Estimation, Listing Tasks, Milestone definition, Cost estimation, Scheduling, and Risk management - PLaMCoD!

mnemonics.title: MoA+D for Modeling Components
mnemonics.content: Modeling = Analysis + Design. Remember: Model has Analysis (what) + Design (how).

acronyms.usage: The five sequential framework activities in software engineering process
Two components of the Modeling framework activity
Two components of the Construction framework activity
Final testing phase often done during Deployment to validate user requirements

visualTips.visualization: Draw a horizontal arrow showing the flow: Communication (left) → Planning → Modeling → Construction → Deployment (right). Each activity feeds into the next.

visualTips.visualize: Think of Modeling as a two-layer structure: Bottom layer = Analysis (what the system must do), Top layer = Design (how the system will do it)
Construction is like building a house: Coding = laying bricks, Testing = checking each brick and the whole structure

analogies.analogy: Like a doctor consultation - asking the patient (stakeholder) about symptoms (requirements) before treatment (building software).
Like creating a travel itinerary - deciding route, time, budget, and contingencies before starting the journey.
Like an architect's blueprints - planning what and how to build before construction begins.
Like manufacturing in a factory - actually producing the product according to specifications.
Like launching a new product in market - making it available to customers and ensuring it works.
